test_suite: Fitwave Android Application Vulnerability Test Cases
description: Automated and manual vulnerability test cases mapping application routes, features, and implementations to common security vulnerabilities.

test_cases.title: Plaintext Password Storage in SharedPreferences
test_cases.description: Verify if the application stores sensitive user authentication credentials (like passwords) in plaintext within the local shared preferences file.
verification_steps: Register a new account on the device / emulator with the password 'UserSecurePassword123'.
Access the device storage shell or device file explorer.
Read the contents of the XML file and search for the key 'password'.
test_cases.expected_result: The password must NOT be stored in plaintext. It should either be encrypted using EncryptedSharedPreferences or not stored locally at all.
test_cases.remediation: Replace standard SharedPreferences with Jetpack Security's EncryptedSharedPreferences to encrypt stored keys and values automatically at rest.

test_cases.title: Weak Password Complexity Verification
test_cases.description: Check if registration allows weak passwords that do not meet standard complexity criteria (e.g. only minimum length check).
verification_steps: Enter a valid name and email.
Enter a password of length 6 consisting of sequential digits (e.g., '123456').
test_cases.expected_result: The application should reject overly simple, sequential, or easily guessable passwords and enforce a password complexity policy (numbers, uppercase, lowercase, special characters).
test_cases.remediation: Implement regex validation in RegisterScreen to enforce stronger complexity requirements (e.g., matching '^(?=.*[0-9])(?=.*[a-z])(?=.*[A-Z])(?=.*[@#$%^&+=])(?=\\S+$).{8,}$').

test_cases.title: Unvalidated URL Redirection in Workout Routine Cards
test_cases.description: Verify whether clicking on a workout routine item redirects to arbitrary external links without URL scheme/host validation, potentially leading to phishing or arbitrary content loading.
verification_steps: Intercept or modify a Workout object's videoUrl to an untrusted scheme or non-youtube domain.
Navigate to the Workout Plan screen.
Click the workout card to trigger the view Intent.
Observe if the system opens arbitrary, non-HTTPS protocols or file schemes.
test_cases.expected_result: The application must sanitize and validate external URLs, ensuring they use 'https' and belong to trusted domains (like youtube.com) before launching an external viewer Intent.
test_cases.remediation: Before executing the ACTION_VIEW intent, validate the URI scheme using `uri.scheme == "https"` and verify that the host belongs to a pre-defined whitelist.

test_cases.title: Lack of Sanitization in Local Database or State Fields
test_cases.description: Ensure profile inputs are sanitized to prevent injection attacks or layout breakage from long/special character payloads.
verification_steps: Go to the Profile / Edit Details screen.
Insert the script tag or the overflow string in the name/phone fields and click save.
Verify if the UI breaks, displays unescaped code, or crashes due to buffer overflow/storage limits.
test_cases.expected_result: Input strings must be properly validated and length-limited prior to local state updates.
test_cases.remediation: Apply length constraint validation and sanitization filters to strip dangerous HTML/XML characters before saving properties.
